name: Italy
description: Southern European country, setting for events in Rome, Milan, Sicily, and Venice.

name: Palazzo Falconieri
description: Palazzo on a promontory on one of the smaller Italian lakes. Meeting place of the Twelve. The east wing serves as a conference room, furnished with an art deco chandelier and a Bugatti bronze panther.

name: Europe
description: Main setting continent for the Killing Eve series.

name: Sicily
description: Island region of southern Italy. Home of the Mafia clans.

name: Palermo
description: Principal point of entry for cocaine into Europe. Greco's base of operations.

name: Calabria
description: Southern Italian region. Home of the 'Ndrangheta.

name: France
description: Western European country, setting for events in Paris, Annecy, Nice, Avignon, and the Cote d'Azur.

name: Paris
description: Main base of operations for Villanelle.

name: 16th Arrondissement
description: Upscale Parisian district. Location of Le Jasmin club.

name: Le Jasmin
description: Private members' club in the 16th Arrondissement of Paris, near Boulevard Suchet and the Bois de Boulogne. Garden bordered by a jasmine trellis.

name: Bois de Boulogne
description: Large park west of Paris, near Le Jasmin.

name: Versailles
description: Location cited in Villanelle's cover story (a fictional flat).

name: Russia
description: Largest country in the world, setting for events in Moscow, Saint Petersburg, Perm, and other cities.

name: Perm Krai
description: Region in central Russia where Villanelle grew up.

name: Perm
description: City where Villanelle attended secondary school and university.

name: Ural Mountains
description: Mountain range. Location of the Dobryanka remand centre.

name: Dobryanka
description: Location of the women's remand centre where Villanelle was held.

name: Dobryanka remand centre
description: Women's remand centre in the Ural Mountains where Villanelle was held, accused of murder.

name: Porte de Passy
description: Area of Villanelle's apartment in Paris.

name: Villanelle apartment Paris
description: Rooftop apartment on the sixth floor of a 1930s building near the Porte de Passy. Reinforced steel front door with custom electronic locks. Rooms in faded sea-green and French blue, with nondescript post-Impressionist paintings. Underground garage with a Vespa and a silver-grey Audi TT Roadster.

name: Chusovaya river
description: Frozen river in Perm Krai. Location of the prefabricated unit where Konstantin recruited Oxana.

name: Chechnya
description: Chechen Republic. Boris Vorontsov served here during the Chechen wars.

name: Sakharov orphanage
description: Orphanage in Perm where Oxana was placed in temporary care after her father's deployment to Chechnya.

name: Municipal Hospital Number 4
description: Hospital in Perm with a psychiatric unit. Oxana was transferred here after setting fire to the orphanage dormitory.

name: Dagestan
description: Russian republic in the North Caucasus. Boris Vorontsov was seconded here.

name: Ekaterinburg
description: City where Villanelle won a gold medal for pistol shooting at the University Games.

name: Rome
description: Capital of Italy.

name: Trastevere
description: Roman district where Bruno De Santis was nearly killed in a hit-and-run.

name: Milan
description: Major city in northern Italy, home to La Scala Theatre Academy.

name: La Scala Theatre Academy
description: Prestigious opera academy in Milan where Franca Farfaglia studies.

name: Teatro Massimo
description: Opera house in Palermo. Franca Farfaglia has an upcoming performance here.

name: Greco farmhouse
description: Salvatore Greco's principal residence. A fortified farmhouse in a hill village outside Palermo, protected by armed bodyguards. Visitors are searched and disarmed.

name: United Kingdom
description: Island nation in Western Europe, comprising England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland.

name: England
description: Largest constituent country of the United Kingdom.

name: Essex coast
description: Lonely, wind-scoured stretch of coastline. Site of Frank's six-week combat training facility: a Nissen hut on a mud-and-shingle island linked by a quarter-mile causeway, formerly a Cold War early-warning station.

name: Germany
description: Central European country.

name: Mittenwald
description: Mountain warfare school. Three weeks of NATO Special Forces escape-and-evasion training.

name: United States
description: North American country.

name: North Carolina
description: US state, location of Fort Bragg military base.

name: Fort Bragg
description: US Army facility in North Carolina. Home to Delta Force's training facility including a killing house for close-quarters combat training. Also site of the Advanced Resistance to Interrogation programme.

name: Ukraine
description: Eastern European country.

name: Kiev
description: Capital of Ukraine. Location of a weapons familiarisation camp to the south.

name: Severka sniper school
description: Urban sniper school built around a deserted Soviet town formerly home to radiation scientists. A ghost town fitted with life-sized target dummies. Run by a private security company near Ekaterinburg.

name: Volgograd
description: Russian city. Site of explosives and toxicology training.

name: Kazan
description: City in Tatarstan, Russia. Home to the Kazan Military Academy, a centre for competitive pistol shooting.

name: London
description: Capital of the United Kingdom. Site of advanced driving and lock-picking training.

name: Belleville
description: Paris suburb. Oxana stayed in a dingy one-star hotel here during steganography training.

name: La Défense
description: Paris business district. An anonymous office building here served as the steganography classroom.

name: Quai Voltaire
description: Left Bank, Paris. Location of Fantine's apartment, where Oxana's style transformation takes place.

name: Piazza Verdi
description: Square in front of Teatro Massimo, with palm trees, bronze lions, and cobblestones.

name: Greece
description: Southern European country. Setting for events in Piraeus.

name: Athens
description: Capital of Greece, near the port of Piraeus.

name: Piraeus
description: Greek port city near Athens. Location of a warehouse district overlooking the harbour.

name: Bayswater
description: London district west of Hyde Park.

name: Montparnasse
description: Parisian district. Villanelle attends a ju-jitsu dojo here.

name: Saint-Cloud
description: West of Paris. Location of an elite shooting club where Villanelle practises marksmanship.

name: Via Volturno
description: Street near the Teatro Massimo in Palermo where Messina parks his MV Agusta motorcycle.

name: Piazza Olivella
description: Square near the Teatro Massimo in Palermo. Location of a cafe frequented by police.

name: Louvre
description: Art museum in Paris. Villanelle contemplates Canova's Psyche Revived by Cupid's Kiss. Café Mollien is on the first floor.

name: Café Mollien
description: Restaurant on the first-floor landing of the Louvre art gallery. Villanelle and Anne-Laure lunch here overlooking the Carousel Gardens.

name: Paradis Latin
description: Cabaret venue in Paris. Anne-Laure Mercier has an affair with a Brazilian dancer who performs there.

name: Carousel Gardens
description: Gardens adjacent to the Louvre, visible from the Café Mollien windows. Almost deserted under a snow-laden grey sky.

name: Pony Club
description: Bar on Ulitsa Pushkina in Perm.

name: Westminster
description: Location of Thames House (MI5 headquarters) on Millbank.

name: Thames House
description: MI5 headquarters on Millbank, Westminster. The P3 office is on the third floor, northernmost wing.

name: High Holborn
description: London district. Location of The Vernon Hotel.

name: The Vernon Hotel
description: Six-storey grey stone hotel on High Holborn. Location of Kedrin's accommodation during his London visit.

name: Red Lion Square
description: Location of Conway Hall where Kedrin is scheduled to address Eurasia UK supporters.

name: Conway Hall
description: Lecture hall in Red Lion Square with wood-panelled walls and a gallery. Kedrin is scheduled to deliver his Eurasia UK speech here.

name: Finchley
description: North London district where Eve and Niko Polastri share a flat.

name: West Hampstead
description: District in north-west London.

name: West Hampstead Bridge Club
description: Bridge club where Niko instructs three evenings a week. Eve and Niko first met here.

name: Poland
description: Eastern European country. Niko Polastri's homeland.

name: Cracow
description: Second-largest city in Poland. Location of the university where Niko Polastri studied Pure and Applied Mathematics.

name: Essex
description: County in south-east England, east of London.

name: Charles de Gaulle airport
description: Paris's main international airport. Villanelle departs from the business-class lounge for London.

name: Cafe La Spezia
description: Cafe on Gray's Inn Road, London. Arranged meeting point for Villanelle and Konstantin.

name: Mayfair
description: Upscale district in central London. Location of Villanelle's safe house on South Audley Street.

name: South Audley Street apartment
description: Top-floor apartment registered to a corporate finance group. Villanelle's safe house during the London operation. Furnished with a white leather Charles Eames chair and floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the city.

name: Nobu
description: Japanese restaurant on Berkeley Street in Mayfair. Known for yellowtail sashimi and wild strawberry sake jelly. Villanelle and Sarah dine here.

name: Hyde Park
description: Large central London park across Park Lane from Mayfair. Villanelle walks the slush-banked paths and encounters a mysterious man on the balustraded bridge across the Serpentine.

name: Paddington Green Police Station
description: High-security Metropolitan Police station. Eve is asked to assist with identification of the arrested suspect here.

name: Avenue Kléber apartment
description: Apartment of Gilles and Anne-Laure Mercier on Avenue Kléber. Site of an elaborate dinner party with a junior minister, hedge fund director, and auction house executive vice president. Gilles's wine cellar, ormolu clocks, and two Boudin oil paintings of Trouville.

name: Arc de Triomphe
description: Iconic Parisian monument at the western end of the Champs-Élysées.

name: Champs-Élysées
description: Famous Parisian avenue running from the Arc de Triomphe to the Place de la Concorde.

name: BQ Optics office
description: Cover office on the second floor above Goodge Street Underground Station, entrance on Tottenham Court Road. Unheated and dusty, with grime-opaque windows, an elderly steel desk, and rust-scarred folding chairs. Used by Richard Edwards as Eve's new operational base for the Kedrin investigation.

name: Goodge Street
description: Area in central London around Goodge Street Underground Station. Location of the BQ Optics cover office.

name: Vauxhall Cross
description: MI6 headquarters on the South Bank of the Thames.

name: Asia
description: Asian continent.

name: China
description: People's Republic of China.

name: Shanghai
description: Major Chinese city and financial hub. Modern skyscrapers dominate the Pudong district while colonial-era buildings line the Bund waterfront.

name: Pudong
description: District in Shanghai east of the Huangpu River, characterised by modern trophy skyscrapers including the Shanghai Tower, World Financial Centre and Jin Mao Tower. Location of the White Dragon crew operational base on Datong Road.

name: White Dragon headquarters
description: Anonymous-looking building on Datong Road in Pudong, fitted with banks of powerful computer servers, high-speed fibre-optic lines and precision air-conditioning systems. Operational base of the White Dragon PLA cyber-warfare unit.

name: The Bund
description: Kilometre-long historic waterfront in Shanghai lined with landmark colonial-era banks and trading houses, now converted to luxury hotels, restaurants and clubs.

name: Old City
description: Historic centre of Shanghai. A maze of narrow, intersecting alleyways teeming with shoppers and tourists, lined with street-food stalls, fake antique shops and laundry-draped lanes.

name: Massachusetts
description: US state on the northeastern seaboard.

name: Dartmouth
description: Town in Massachusetts. Location of a private seafront estate.

name: Dangfeng Road establishment
description: Three-storey corner building on Dangfeng Road in Pudong serving as a fetish and massage establishment. Entry via keypad code. First floor hosts conventional pleasures; second floor is a specialist medical-themed suite with a gurney, monitors, ventilator and surgical instruments.

name: French Concession
description: Former French colonial district of Shanghai. Upscale residential area with high-rise apartments overlooking the neighbourhood rooftops.

name: Tchaikovsky Theatre
description: Opera house in Perm, named after Tchaikovsky. Belle Epoque foyer. Tea room with duck-egg green walls, russet velvet curtains, and twin chandeliers. Balcony with back-row seats (best acoustics). Eve attends Manon Lescaut here with Anna Leonova, and Oxana reappears alive during the interval.

name: Huangpu River
description: Major river flowing through Shanghai, separating Pudong from the historic city centre.

name: The Aquarium
description: Exclusive nightclub in the basement of a former private bank on the North Bund. Entrance by personal introduction only. Features a floor-to-ceiling shark tank with hammerheads, reef sharks, and tiger sharks. Art deco bar. Owned by Alice Mao.

name: Sea Bird Hotel
description: Budget hotel on Suzhou Creek in Shanghai. Described as discreet. Eve and Simon's accommodation during their visit.

name: Peninsula Hotel
description: Luxury art deco hotel on The Bund, opened in 2009 as the first new building on the Bund in seventy years. Features a pillared ivory-and-gold lobby with vast carpets and white-uniformed bellboys.

name: Putua Parlour
description: Warehouse-style kitchen and restaurant supply store on Changhua Lu. Stocks every imaginable appliance and vessel including pans, steamers, woks, and an entire aisle of cleavers.

name: East Nanjing Road
description: One of Shanghai's busiest thoroughfares. Major commercial street gridlocked with traffic.

name: Paramount dance hall
description: Grand art deco ballroom from the 1930s, topped by a cascading neon spire. A famous Shanghai landmark where gangsters, high society, and beautiful women once came to dance. Features a gilt-and-red-plush dance hall on the fourth floor with a cantilevered dance floor and live music.

name: Star Bar
description: Expat bar in Shanghai with zebra-skin upholstered chairs, emerald-green stingray-skin walls, sub-pornographic paintings, a black marble fireplace, and a vast Fortuny-style chandelier. Described as absurd, alluring, and vaguely satanic.

name: Jingan
description: Shanghai district near the Art Theatre.

name: Suzhou Creek
description: Waterway running through central Shanghai, also known as Wusong River. Nan Suzhou Lu runs along its southern bank.

name: Hampshire
description: County in southern England.

name: Bullington
description: Village in Hampshire, location of the Dever Research Station.

name: Dever Research Station
description: Top-secret government facility in former Logistics Corps barracks outside Bullington in Hampshire. Base for E Squadron. Set in sixty acres of chain-link-fenced grounds with an underground firing range.

name: 6eme Arrondissement
description: Left Bank Paris district. Location of Anne-Laure's apartment on rue de Vaugirard.

name: Anne-Laure apartment
description: Sixth-floor single-bedroom apartment on rue de Vaugirard, inherited by Anne-Laure. Used for private liaisons away from her husband Gilles.

name: Marais
description: Historic Paris district known for vintage clothes stores, frequented by Villanelle and Anne-Laure.

name: Croydon
description: South London borough. Where Lance Pope lives alone in a rented flat.

name: Issy-les-Moulineaux
description: Suburb south of Paris. Location of a heliport used for covert transport operations.

name: Issy-les-Moulineaux heliport
description: Heliport south of Paris. Pick-up point for covert helicopter transport, located on a rectangle of tarmac enclosed by warehouses.

name: Annecy
description: City in south-eastern France. The Mont Blanc airfield is used as a transit point for covert transport.

name: Annecy Mont Blanc airfield
description: Small airfield in south-eastern France. Transit point where Villanelle transfers from helicopter to a North Star Learjet.

name: Scherbanka
description: Area in southern Ukraine with a small private airfield.

name: Odessa
description: Major Ukrainian port city on the Black Sea.

name: Muswell Hill
description: North London suburb. Location of Dennis Cradle's house.

name: Mazeppa restaurant
description: Michelin-starred restaurant on Dover Street, Mayfair.

name: Moldovanka
description: District in Odessa, location of the Bird Market area.

name: Fontanka
description: Area near Odessa where Rinat Yevtukh owns a well-secured villa.

name: Bird Market cafe
description: Dingy cafe in the Moldovanka district of Odessa, with yellowish lighting, faded travel posters, and a blackboard advertising daily specials.

name: Canary Wharf
description: East London financial district. Where Penny Cradle's law firm is based.

name: FOB farmhouse
description: Disused farmhouse two miles north-west of Fontanka. Forward operating base with trestle tables, maps, architectural plans, and weapons boxes in an outbuilding.

name: Yevtukh villa
description: Ostentatious three-storey palazzo with pillars, balustrades, and a steeply pitched tile roof. Surrounded by a chain-link fence with a guarded electronic gate, set in grounds of half-a-dozen acres.

name: Moscow
description: Capital of Russia.

name: Vnukovo airport
description: Airport south-west of Moscow.

name: Cradle house
description: Residential home of Dennis Cradle in Muswell Hill, north London.

name: Tottenham Court Road
description: Central London road near Eve's Goodge Street office.

name: Rue du Faubourg St Honoré
description: Prestigious shopping street in Paris, home to luxury fashion houses.

name: Soho
description: Central London district known for entertainment, restaurants, and nightlife.

name: Dover Street
description: Street in Mayfair, London. Location of exclusive restaurants and dining establishments.

name: North Circular Road
description: Major London ring road. Near the Tesco where Cradle is interrogated.

name: Tesco Muswell Hill
description: Tesco superstore car park near the North Circular Road. Lance and Billy bring Cradle here for a voice-changer phone call with Eve.

name: Cap d'Antibes
description: Exclusive area on the French Riviera. Location of Cradle's secret apartment Les Asphodeles.

name: Finchley Road
description: Major road in north London running through Finchley and West Hampstead. Location of Finchley Road tube station.

name: Les Asphodeles
description: Three-bedroom apartment in Cap d'Antibes purchased by Dennis Cradle with Twelve money. Part of his secret double life.

name: Port Vauban marina
description: Marina in Antibes where Cradle's yacht Babydoll is moored.

name: Venice
description: City in northern Italy built on a lagoon. Historic maritime republic known for canals, gondolas, and architecture.

name: Danieli Hotel
description: Luxury hotel in Venice. Rinat Yevtukh stays in the Doge's Suite.

name: San Marco
description: Central Venice district with the famous piazza. Shops and pasticceria.

name: Murano
description: Island in the Venetian lagoon, famous for its glassmaking. Yevtukh and Goraya visited it as tourists.

name: Eve and Niko flat
description: One-bedroom rented flat on Finchley Road where Eve and Niko live. Features a small paved area they call the garden.

name: Giudecca
description: Island south of central Venice, visible from the lagoon along the route into the open water.

name: Ottagone Falieri
description: Octagonal island fortification in the Venice lagoon, built to protect Venice from invaders. Nominally owned by the Falieri family. Now largely a ruin, accessible only by boat.

name: Marco Polo airport
description: Venice's main airport on the Italian mainland, named after the Venetian explorer.

name: Poveglia
description: Abandoned island in the Venice lagoon. Former plague quarantine station in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, later home to a mental institution. Now deserted and reputed to be haunted; tourist craft rarely venture there. A narrow canal divides the island into two halves.

name: Lido
description: Barrier island in the Venice lagoon, separating the lagoon from the Adriatic Sea. Known for its beaches and luxury hotels.

name: Hotel Excelsior
description: Luxury hotel on the Lido in Venice. Features a pillared Moorish-style restaurant in shades of white and ivory, with a terrace overlooking the lagoon.

name: Chipping Norton
description: Town in Oxfordshire, England. Described as a wealthy area popular with politicians and affluent families.

name: Spain
description: Country in southern Europe.

name: Malaga
description: City in southern Spain. Setting for a tennis holiday where Dennis Cradle was first approached by Rem Bakker as part of his recruitment by the Twelve.

name: Pall Mall
description: Street in central London. Location of an exclusive wine club where Sergei was introduced to Dennis Cradle over a bottle of Domaine de la Romanee-Conti.

name: Micheldever
description: Area in Hampshire near the A303 motorway. Scene of Villanelle's interception of Eve and Cradle, disguised as a police motorcycle officer.

name: Surrey
description: County in south-east England.

name: Weybridge
description: Town in Surrey, England, situated near the River Wey.

name: River Wey weir
description: Weir-pool on the River Wey near Weybridge, fringed with bullrushes and flag-iris. Accessible via a tree-lined riverside path.

name: Gare du Nord
description: Major Paris railway station and Eurostar terminal. One of the busiest stations in Europe.

name: Eastleigh
description: Town in Hampshire, England. Location of Hampshire Constabulary headquarters.

name: Place de la Concorde
description: Monumental square in central Paris. A hotel overlooking the square is the setting for an encounter Villanelle abandons.

name: La Muette
description: Residential quarter of the 16th Arrondissement. Villanelle uses its narrow streets for counter-surveillance.

name: Avenue Victor Hugo
description: Street in the 16th Arrondissement where Villanelle parks her scooter near a club.

name: Villanelle apartment Paris
description: Rooftop apartment on the sixth floor with a short flight of stairs from the lift. Walls of faded sea-green and French blue, mid-century furniture including Eileen Gray pieces, and minor post-Impressionist paintings. Underground car park with a Vespa and a silver-grey Audi. East-facing plate-glass window with a panoramic view of Paris. Electronic locking system. Service charges paid from a corporate account in Geneva through untraceable front companies.

name: Knock Tom
description: Second World War anti-aircraft marine platform in the North Sea, roughly ten miles east of the Essex coast. A grey rectangle supported by two trunk-like columns, with a helipad at one end. Three converted shipping containers lashed to the decking with steel hawsers serve as the main living and working area. The nearest container has double-glazed windows framing sea and sky views, a trestle table with six folding chairs, a microwave, chest freezer, and kettle. A tray holds jars of honey, Marmite, and strawberry jam. A bookshelf has paperback thrillers by Mick Herron and Andrei Kivinov, and a hardback Birds of the North Sea. Quarters are in the platform legs (north and south), with concrete cabins and metal bunk beds. No WiFi, total isolation. Used as a training base for the Twelve's sniper mission.

name: Lambeth
description: South London district. Location of the Metropolitan Police Forensics Laboratory.

name: Met Police Forensics Lab
description: Metropolitan Police Forensics Laboratory in Lambeth. Equipped for DNA tape-lifts, gas chromatography, mass spectrometry, and graphology analysis.

name: Calle Vallaresso
description: Street in the San Marco area of Venice. Location of the Van Diest jewellery boutique and the Zucchetti patisserie.

name: Zucchetti
description: Small and expensive Venetian patisserie specialising in galani, three doors from the Van Diest boutique on Calle Vallaresso.

name: Van Diest boutique Venice
description: Van Diest jewellery boutique on Calle Vallaresso, at the eastern end of Piazza San Marco.

name: Austria
description: Federal state in Central Europe.

name: High Tauern
description: Mountain range of the Eastern Alps in Austria, home to the Hohe Tauern National Park.

name: Felsnadel
description: Luxury mountain hotel in the High Tauern, high above the snowline. Designed by a famous architect and accessible only by helicopter. Max Linder books it annually for himself and his political associates.

name: Innsbruck
description: Capital of Tyrol, Austria. Location of the staffing agency that supplies personnel to the Felsnadel hotel.

name: Piazza San Marco
description: Venice's main public square, surrounded by arcaded buildings, the Basilica, and the Campanile. Paved with stone, home to flocks of pigeons.

name: Santa Croce
description: Sestiere of Venice. Location of the questura (central police station) on the Ponte della Liberta, with blue police launches moored at its river jetty and steel security barriers at the street entrance.

name: Dorsoduro
description: Southern sestiere of Venice. Location of Palazzo Forlani and the Accademia Bridge.

name: La Fenice
description: Venice's historic opera house, Teatro La Fenice. Eve spots a boutique nearby while walking in the area.

name: Accademia Bridge
description: Bridge over the Grand Canal in Venice connecting San Marco and Dorsoduro. Landmark used by Giovanna to direct Eve to Palazzo Forlani.

name: Palazzo Forlani
description: Venetian palazzo on the Dorsoduro, a hundred metres past the Accademia Bridge. Venue for Umberto Zeni's jewellery collection reception.

name: Royal Free Hospital
description: NHS hospital in Hampstead, north London. A major acute care and teaching hospital.

name: Herzog-Friedrich-Strasse
description: Historic street in central Innsbruck with illuminated buildings framing the distant mountains.

name: Gasthof Lili
description: Small inn in Innsbruck. A narrow room on the first floor serves as a base for studying mission plans and learning German.

name: Brauhaus Adler
description: Brewery pub on the Schlossergasse in central Innsbruck. Warm and beery inside, with high noise levels.

name: Teufelskamp
description: Mountain in the High Tauern on which the Felsnadel hotel stands, at approximately 2,500 metres above sea level.

name: Questura di Venezia
description: Venice police headquarters in the Santa Croce district. Workplace of Questore Armando Trevisan.

name: Innsbruck heliport
description: Flugrettungszentrum, Innsbruck's heliport. Departure lounge where Max Linder's invited guests gather and sip Pol Roger champagne before helicopter transfer to the Felsnadel.

name: Marbella
description: Coastal city in southern Spain on the Costa del Sol.

name: Eton
description: Eton College, prestigious English boarding school founded in 1440. Tony Kent and Dennis Cradle studied here together; Kent was Cradle's fag (personal servant in the school's tradition).

name: Sandhurst
description: Royal Military Academy Sandhurst. Tony Kent trained here after failing MI5 selection, receiving a commission as a second lieutenant in the Royal Logistics Corps before serving two tours in Iraq.

name: Maison Joliot
description: Historic Parisian perfumery on the Rue du Faubourg Saint-Honoré, owned by the same family for generations. Its most expensive range is Poésies, comprising four fragrances: Kyrielle, Rondine, Triolet, and Villanelle. The Villanelle fragrance is distinguished by a scarlet ribbon at the neck, added after the Comtesse du Barry was guillotined in 1793.

name: Fontanka
description: Area approximately fifteen kilometres outside Odessa. Location of a luxury property owned by Rinat Yevtukh.

name: Oxford Street
description: Main commercial street of London. Eve and Richard meet at a department-store café on the fourth floor, overlooking the street.

name: Afghanistan
description: Central Asian country. Setting for the Soviet-Afghan War in the 1980s. Richard Edwards served here for MI6 and met Konstantin Orlov at the Russian embassy in Kabul.

name: Kabul
description: Capital of Afghanistan. Richard Edwards was posted here for MI6 and met Konstantin Orlov at the Russian embassy. Together they visited a falconry camp in the desert outside the city, accessible via Dar-al-Aman Road and the Bagram road.

name: Cyprus
description: Mediterranean island nation south of Turkey.

name: Cotswolds
description: Area of rolling hills in south-central England, known for picturesque stone-built villages.

name: Redditch
description: Town in Worcestershire, England.

name: Aldeburgh
description: Coastal town in Suffolk, east England.

name: Andropov Academy
description: Spy training school outside Moscow, originally run by the KGB and later by the SVR. Yuri Modin was a guest lecturer, teaching the Active Measures course (subversion, disinformation, and assassination). Konstantin Orlov trained here, graduating circa 1992.

name: Sheremetyevo
description: Moscow international airport. Destination of Eve and Lance's flight from Heathrow for their Russia investigation.

name: Cosmos Hotel
description: Hotel in Moscow where Eve and Lance are to stay during their Russia mission. Arranged by Oleg.

name: Whitchurch
description: Town in Hampshire, England.

name: Prospekt Mira
description: Broad avenue in Moscow. The Cosmos Hotel is located here. The VDNKh exhibition complex extends across the road.

name: VDNKh
description: All-Russia Exhibition Centre. A vast and melancholy park built in the mid-twentieth century to celebrate Soviet economic achievements. Triumphal arches with flaking columns frame empty air. Neo-classical pavilions stand padlocked and deserted. Visitors huddle on benches staring into the middle distance. Above it all, an almost artificially blue sky and scudding white clouds.

name: Monument to the Conquerors of Space
description: Hundred-metre-high representation in shining titanium of a rocket rising on its exhaust plume. Located near the VDNKh park. A kebab vendor sets up his stand at the base. Eve recalls the dog Laika sent up alone into space.

name: VDNKh Metro Station
description: Moscow metro station accessed via a circular pillared atrium. A palatial underground concourse lit with ornate chandeliers. White marble walls and vaulted ceiling, with archways faced in green mosaic leading to the railway platforms.

name: Lubyanka
description: Vast and cheerless edifice in ochre brick dominating a wide square in central Moscow. Former headquarters of the KGB, now occupied by the FSB. The rear is a wasteland of building works and litter, wire grilles over grimy windows. A low doorway surmounted by a hammer and sickle in carved stone leads to the underground cells.

name: Reichenau
description: Locality near Innsbruck where Villanelle is treated at a private clinic after the Felsnadel explosion.

name: Reichenau Clinic
description: Private clinic in Reichenau, near Innsbruck. Very discreet facility frequented by post-operative cosmetic surgery patients (some sort of treatment where they pack your face in snow). Villanelle recovers here from blast-wave concussion and whiplash, checked in by Anton as a French tourist after a driving accident.

name: Butyrka
description: Historic Moscow prison, a couple of miles from the Lubyanka. Larissa Farmanyants is detained here after her arrest by the FSB.

name: Ostafyevo Airport
description: Private airport in Moscow. Eve departs from here on a Dassault Falcon jet with Gazprom executives, fast-tracked through customs by Tikhomirov's driver.

name: Kalinka Restaurant
description: Restaurant in the Cosmos Hotel, Moscow.

name: Whitlock and Jones
description: Pharmaceutical and medical supply shop on Welbeck Street, near Harley Street and Wimpole Street.

name: Café Torino
description: Café on Finchley Road, north London.

name: London City Airport
description: London airport. Eve lands here on her return from Moscow.

name: Chiswick
description: District in west London overlooking the Thames. Location of the art gallery where Amanda Edwards exhibits her paintings and drawings.

name: Edwards Gallery Chiswick
description: Ground-floor art gallery in a Georgian house with a sunlit brick frontage, identified only by a small silver plaque. A wide bow window overlooks the Thames. Amanda Edwards exhibits watercolours and drawings here.

name: Baltic Sea
description: Body of water between Scandinavia and northern continental Europe.

name: Lincolnshire
description: County on the east coast of England. Location of the port of Immingham.

name: Welbeck Street
description: Street in central London, Marylebone area. Location of the Whitlock and Jones pharmaceutical and medical supply shop, near Harley Street and Wimpole Street.

name: Harley Street
description: Street in central London famous for its concentration of private medical and surgical specialists.

name: Immingham
description: Industrial port in Lincolnshire, on the North Sea coast. Eve and Villanelle stow away here aboard the Kirovo-Chepetsk. They meet Igor in a fake Irish pub outside the terminal.

name: St Petersburg
description: Major Russian city on the Baltic Sea. Eve and Villanelle arrive here by container ship from Immingham. One year after the Bolshoi events, they live in an apartment in the outer suburbs under new identities.

name: Smolensk
description: City in western Russia.

name: Industrialny Cemetery
description: Public cemetery in the Industrialny district of Perm.

name: River Wey
description: River in Surrey, England, flowing through Weybridge.

name: Gulf of Riga
description: Inlet of the Baltic Sea, bordered by Latvia and Estonia.

name: Ugolnaya Harbour
description: Harbour in St Petersburg where the Kirovo-Chepetsk docks. Container terminal used for clandestine disembarkation.

name: Prekrasnaya Nevesta Warehouse
description: Cathedral-sized industrial warehouse in St Petersburg owned by the bridal gown company Prekrasnaya Nevesta. Vast interior filled with ranks of industrial garment-rails hung with wedding dresses — an army of ghostly brides. Strip lights suspended from rusting joists. Steel doors with a judas gate. Office above the floor accessible by stairway.

name: Karelia
description: Republic in north-west Russia, bordering Finland. Known for forests and lakes.

name: Avtovo
description: District of St Petersburg characterised by massive neoclassical Soviet-era buildings, formerly residences of senior Communist Party officials. Dasha Kvariani's apartment is located here. The rumble and hiss of traffic and the clanking of trams are audible from the upper floors.

name: Dasha's Apartment
description: Dasha Kvariani's third-floor apartment in a massive neoclassical block in Avtovo. The building was once very grand but has been in decline for decades. Worn fittings, creaking lift, clanking plumbing. Tall windows overlook a broad highway.

name: Stachek Prospekt
description: Main avenue in the Avtovo district of St Petersburg. Grimy yellow-brown buildings dusted with sparkling frozen snowfall. A short distance down from Dasha's apartment. Traffic rumble and tram clatter audible from nearby windows.

name: Kometa
description: Former cinema converted into a second-hand store on Stachek Prospekt, Avtovo. The auditorium holds rank after rank of clothing stalls: goth, punk, old theatre costumes, military regalia, fetish-wear, and home-made jewellery. Art deco chandeliers hang overhead. Musty, cloying smell. Eve and Oxana reinvent their wardrobes here.

name: Aviatorov Park
description: Public park in Avtovo with a frozen lake girded by dark skeletal trees. On the far shore, a Soviet monument stands on a promontory: a MiG fighter aircraft leaping into the sky, arrested at the moment of take-off. Near-deserted in winter with flurries of snow.

name: Kupchino
description: Peripheral district south of St Petersburg, from which the Kupchino Bratva takes its name. Location of Kupchino metro station. The Pakhan's residence on Malaya Balkanskaya Ulitsa is here.

name: Proletarskaya
description: District of St Petersburg where the Elizarova Bathhouse is located, a regular venue for the Pakhan Asmat Dzabrati.

name: Nevsky Prospekt
description: Famous main avenue of St Petersburg. Location of a clinic where the Pakhan receives rejuvenation treatments and Zoya gets Botox injections. Also houses the Pakhan's private cardiologist.

name: Malaya Balkanskaya Ulitsa
description: Street in the Kupchino district of St Petersburg, near Kupchino metro station. Location of Pakhan Asmat Dzabrati's massive grey seventeen-storey apartment block, where he owns several apartments occupied by bodyguards, his ex-wife Yelena, and his sister Rushana.

name: Elizarova Bathhouse
description: Historic bathhouse in the Proletarskaya district of St Petersburg, built in Tsarist times, over a hundred years old. Mosaics and classical statues. Interior: changing room with lockers, steam rooms with fire-boxes (giant ovens with hot rocks), cooling-off room with steel pillars and marble slabs for massage, a cold plunge pool (one person, metre and a half deep), and a tea room with a samovar. The Pakhan Asmat Dzabrati frequents it regularly on men-only days.

name: Zarina Restaurant
description: Ossetian restaurant in St Petersburg where the Pakhan Dzabrati holds meetings with his Kupchino Bratva brigadiers. Features a private room reserved for the Pakhan and his guests.

name: Moskovskaya
description: Area of St Petersburg around the Moskovskaya metro station, two stops north of Kupchino on the Metro. Pick-up point for the Gazelle ambulance used in the Pakhan's assassination, parked outside Alfa Bank.

name: Chuvashia
description: Federal republic in central Russia, located in the Volga region. Home to the Chuvash people, a Turkic ethnic group.

name: Kungur ice caves
description: Tourist attraction near the town of Kungur in the Perm region. Underground caves with ice crystals, shining ice pillars and waterfalls, and rock pools illuminated by coloured lights.

name: Ramenki
description: District in the south-western part of Moscow. The characters follow a motorway exit to Ramenki on arriving in the city. Location of the massive Stalinist building housing the Twelve's headquarters.

name: Novozybkov
description: Town in the Bryansk Oblast of western Russia, near the Ukrainian border. Severely affected by the Chernobyl nuclear disaster.

name: Twelve Moscow Headquarters
description: Massive Stalinist block in the Ramenki district of Moscow. Grey frontage pierced with archways, extending the length of an entire street. Towers so tall they vanish into the night sky. Huge dimly lit atrium with marble pillars and classical details. The twelfth floor is accessed via a code-controlled glass-and-steel lift. Armoured-glass windows overlook the Moscow river winding between snowy parks. A huge Salvador Dali painting of a tiger dominates the entrance. Corridors hung with abstract paintings in ivory, scarlet, and vermilion. Staff in paramilitary uniforms and business suits. Dove-grey rooms with bronze statuettes. Serves as the operational headquarters of the Twelve.

name: Belgium
description: European country. Intermediate stop on the journey from Russia to Knock Tom.

name: Ostend
description: Coastal city in Belgium. The group lands at Ostend-Bruges airport by Learjet from Moscow before transferring to a Super Puma helicopter for the flight over the North Sea to Knock Tom.

name: North Sea
description: Body of water between the British Isles and continental Europe. The Knock Tom platform stands roughly ten miles off the Essex coast.

name: Lefortovo
description: FSB detention and interrogation centre in Moscow. Eve mentions it to Charlie as the place they would be taken if the FSB discovered their involvement in the Twelve's operation.

name: Neglinka River Underground
description: Underground tunnel network beneath Moscow, built around the Neglinka river which was diverted underground in the eighteenth century. Contains brick tunnels, iron walkways, weeping walls, sulphurous draughts, mushroom growths, and Stalinist-era skeletons. Inhabited by addicts, ex-convicts, and hermits. Features a weir with a waterfall concealing a passage to the D-6 network.

name: D-6 Underground Network
description: Stalin's secret metro line beneath Moscow. Accessed through a hidden passage behind a waterfall in the Neglinka tunnels. Features a huge cylindrical shaft with zigzagging staircases descending over a hundred metres, coated in flaking red anti-rust paint. Guarded by GUSP (former 15th Directorate of the KGB).

name: Efremova Station
description: D-6 underground station with a deserted platform, glazed-tile walls bearing a bronze hammer and sickle a metre high, and an enamel sign reading D6-EFREMOVA. Part of Stalin's secret metro network.

name: Red Square
description: Iconic public square in the heart of Moscow, bordered by the Kremlin, GUM department store, and St Basils Cathedral. On New Years Eve it fills with revellers, pop music, and an outdoor ice rink.

name: Kremlin
description: Fortified complex bordering Red Square. The eastern wall features historic Gothic towers, one of which is used as a concealed firing position by the Twelve.

name: Kremlin East Tower
description: Small octagonal chamber atop an ancient tower on the eastern Kremlin wall. Narrow Gothic windows, uncleaned for years, some panes cracked or missing. Accessed via the Nikolskaya corridor from the D-6 network. Provides a line of sight over Red Square to the Bolshoi Theatre at 713 metres.

name: Bolshoi Theatre
description: Neoclassical theatre with eight pillars and three double entrance doors. On New Years Eve it stages Schelkunchik (The Nutcracker). Standing 713 metres from the Kremlin East Tower firing position.

name: Azov Hotel
description: Small one-star hotel on a side street off Ulitsa Pushkina in central Perm. Eve stays here after being sent by the FSB. Small room with an iron bed, crocheted blanket, and noisy refrigerator.

name: Komsomolsky Prospekt
description: Main avenue of Perm where Eve walks during her solitary days of grief. Lined with department stores, families in coats and snow boots.

name: River Kama
description: Major tributary of the Volga flowing through Perm. Eve walks along it during her solitary outings.

name: Cafe Skazka
description: Cafe across from the Azov Hotel in Perm. Warm, steamy atmosphere, run by a middle-aged couple and their daughter. Eve lunches here daily during her stay.

name: Sadovaya Ulitsa
description: Street in Saint Petersburg near Oxana university. Dasha Kvariani opens a nightclub here a year after the Bolshoi events.

name: Cafe Singer
description: Cafe above Dom Knigi (House of Books) on Nevsky Prospekt, Saint Petersburg. Eve and Oxana lunch here in the final scene of Book 3.

name: Neva River
description: Main river of Saint Petersburg. Eve and Oxana walk down to the embankment and gaze over the frozen Neva in the fading light of a Russian winter afternoon — the closing image of Book 3.

name: Pokrovskaya Hospital
description: Hospital in Saint Petersburg.

name: Sochi
description: Modern coastal city on the Black Sea in southern Russia.

name: Black Sea
description: Body of water between south-eastern Europe and western Asia.

name: Gulf of Finland
description: Body of water visible from Eve and Oxana apartment in the western suburbs of Saint Petersburg. Extends to the horizon beyond a four-lane highway.

name: St Petersburg University
description: Saint Petersburg State University, where Oxana studies linguistics.

name: Griboyedov Canal Embankment
description: Embankment along the Griboyedov Canal in Saint Petersburg.

name: Aurora Cinema
description: Grand but chilly cinema in Saint Petersburg, an occasional leisure spot for Eve and Oxana.

name: Udelnaya Market
description: Market in Saint Petersburg where Oxana buys inexpensive clothes, including a knock-off North Face jacket.

name: House of Books
description: Dom Knigi, large bookshop on Nevsky Prospekt. Well heated in winter, a regular haunt for Eve and Oxana.

name: Apokalipsis Club
description: Exclusive invitation-only club on the Griboyedov Canal Embankment in Saint Petersburg. Frequented by inhabitants of the mir teney (shadow world).

name: Peterhof
description: Suburb of Saint Petersburg, famous for its imperial palaces and parks.

name: English Park
description: English-style landscaped park at Peterhof with a lake and benches overlooking the water. Eve and Oxana spend an afternoon here.

name: Komfort Laundromat
description: Coin-operated launderette in the western suburbs of Saint Petersburg. Eve and Oxana do Saturday laundry here among competitive babushkas.

name: Kovenskiy Lane
description: Lane in central Saint Petersburg where a pizza place is located. Oxana uses it as her cover story for the Apokalipsis visit.

name: Demidov Bridge
description: Bridge over the Griboyedov Canal near the entrance to Apokalipsis Club. Patriot and Tiger SUVs disgorge guests on the wet cobbles.

name: State Russian Museum
description: State Russian Museum on Arts Square in Saint Petersburg. Former imperial palace with a grand staircase and pillared balcony. Houses Ilya Repin portraits including Alexandra Feodorovna. Site of Villanelle meeting with Sergei.

name: Forestry Academy Park
description: Park of the Forestry Academy in the Vyborgsky district of Saint Petersburg. Shadowed paths wind between dense trees. Contains a grey stone monument marking the site of a nineteenth-century duel in which both combatants were killed. Site of a meeting with a Twelve intermediary.

name: Lesnoy Prospekt
description: Avenue in the Vyborgsky district of Saint Petersburg, near the Forestry Academy Park. Villanelle exits the metro here.

name: Ventnor Road Safe House
description: MI6 safe house in North Finchley. Decade-old magnolia emulsion, stained carpet, stale-smelling fridge with cracked salad drawer. Narrow single bed with worn pillowcases and a My Little Pony duvet cover. Intended for informants and witness-protection people, assigned to Villanelle by administrative error — she was supposed to go to Belsize Park.

name: Harrods
description: Luxury department store in Knightsbridge, London. Site of the meeting between Villanelle and Maria at the Guerlain perfume counter. Has a cafe where Maria orders green salad and Villanelle orders a full English breakfast.

name: Pulkovo Airport
description: Saint Petersburg airport. Villanelle departs from here on a British Airways flight to London Heathrow.

name: Victoria
description: London district around Victoria railway station.

name: Transept House
description: MI6 outstation behind Victoria railway station. Anonymous office building with heavy security: X-ray scanners, body scans, aggressive pat-downs, armed Metropolitan Police with submachine guns. Offices overlook roofs of Victoria and Pimlico. Desiccated rubber plants, triple-glazed windows, elderly Gestetner photocopier.

name: Wiltshire
description: County in south-western England. Location of Teffont Castle.

name: Teffont Castle
description: MI6 safe house compound in Wiltshire countryside near Teffont village. Accessed via a narrow lane through wheat fields and a beech coppice, with a flint-walled lodge and road barrier manned by an armed guard (holstered Glock). The original castle was abandoned four centuries ago after the owner was executed for treason and is now a ruin. Eve and Niko Polastri are housed in a nearby farmhouse on a low ridge, with wisteria and jasmine trained up the side.

name: M25
description: London orbital motorway (ring road). Villanelle and Balice drive along it heading for Wiltshire after Max s killing at Transept House.

name: Les Pivoines
description: Villa of Ron Tiberius on the French Riviera. Built in the early twentieth century; both villa and gardens are listed as exceptional by the French Ministry of Culture. Not open to the public, but groups are admitted to the gardens by special arrangement.

name: El Tiburón
description: Superyacht belonging to Ron Tiberius.

name: Salisbury
description: City in Wiltshire, famous for its cathedral spire. Villanelle and Balice stop here after Teffont: Balice goes to hospital to get her torn earlobes stitched, Villanelle walks to see the cathedral. They stay at a dismal bed and breakfast with nylon sheets.

name: St Pancras International
description: Eurostar terminal in London. Departure point for the Ossa and Pelion horticultural tour to France. Crowded with passengers at 7am.

name: Chateau de Faycelles
description: Neoclassical chateau built on the orders of Queen Marie Antoinette, about an hour drive from Paris. Gardens laid out in the Anglo-Chinese style. Features an orangery with a painted ceiling, a formal rose garden with arbours and pergolas, and a Latin inscription over the entrance: Contra vim mortis non crescit salvia in hortis. Not all of the estate is open to the public, but Ossa and Pelion secured special access.

name: Nice
description: City on the French Riviera, near Ron Tiberius villa Les Pivoines. Sylvie the porn actress is local to Nice. Operational base for MI6 assassination plan.

name: St-Sepulcre
description: Quarter near the old port in Nice. Location of the cafe where Myrtha Willis recruits young women for Ron Tiberius. Sylvie was approached here.

name: Avignon
description: City in Provence, France. The Ossa and Pelion tour visits the Palace of the Popes and the Papal Gardens.

name: Palace of the Popes
description: Historic papal palace in Avignon. The tour group has lunch here before visiting the Papal Gardens.

name: Poisy
description: Village in the Provencal countryside. Location of the Pavillon du Rossignol and the tour hotel.

name: Pavillon du Rossignol
description: Pretty two-storey building faced in apricot stucco, with shutters of faded green. Property of the Comtesse de Quincy, near the village of Poisy. Garden is a memorial to her late husband. Shaded terrace with lavender beds and cicadas. Site of Johnny Fernandes revealing himself as a Twelve handler.

name: Grasse
description: Town in Provence, world capital of perfume. The Ossa and Pelion tour visits the Chanel perfumery and flower fields outside the town.

name: Chanel Perfumery
description: Chanel perfumery and flower farm outside Grasse. Chateau, distillery, and wet fields of roses and jasmine. Scene of Balice showing Villanelle the Eve-Niko vow renewal photo, the phone thrown into a flower field, and Balice slapping Villanelle.

name: Cap Ferrat
description: Peninsula on the French Riviera between Nice and Monaco. Elegant tour hotel with umbrella pines, sea-facing terraces, and Tiffany concession. Final stop before the visit to Les Pivoines. Villanelle meets Johnny Fernandes at sunset over Negronis to discuss plans that differ from Balice instructions.

name: Villefranche-sur-Mer
description: Coastal town on the French Riviera. The tour minibus winds through on the way to Cap Ferrat. Translucent blue-green sea and pale crescent beach visible from the road.

name: London Bridge
description: Bridge over the Thames connecting Borough High Street to the City of London.

name: River Thames
description: Major river flowing through central London.

name: West Sussex
description: County in southern England between the South Downs and the sea.

name: Barnham
description: Small town in West Sussex, between the South Downs and the sea. Eve and Villanelle hide on a thousand-acre farm nearby.

name: Shepherd's Hut
description: Green-painted wagon on cast-iron wheels on a thousand-acre farm near Barnham. Has a tiny wood stove and narrow bed.

name: Indian Military and Sporting Club
description: Exclusive club on St James Street, a few minutes from Piccadilly. Only a small brass plaque at the entrance. Rooms with portraits, regimental silver, and equestrian trophies. Johnny is a member as a former Third Lancers cavalryman. Venue for the Dominik mission briefing.

name: Kensington Palace Gardens
description: London street once known as Millionaires Row, now home to billionaires. Location of the Dominik mansion, overlooking Hyde Park.

name: Mount Street
description: Street in Mayfair, London. Apartment used as a bolthole by Villanelle and Eve, provided by Johnny and the Twelve. Identified by Balice MI6 surveillance team.

name: Park Lane
description: Major road along the eastern edge of Hyde Park. Contains underground parking garages used by the Twelve to switch vehicles and evade MI6 surveillance.

name: Dominik Mansion
description: Massive white Italianate palazzo in Kensington Palace Gardens. Home of the Dominik family. Contains dining rooms, drawing rooms, billiards room, at least two libraries, a possible ballroom, and an unexplored third floor. Gated entrance with guards.

name: Clapham
description: South London district where Balice and Charlie St Clair live in a heavily mortgaged terraced house.

name: Hillingdon
description: West London district. Location of Bilyana Ilieva flat, where Balice wakes after a one-night stand. Far from central London with Uber difficulties and a bus to Uxbridge.

name: Romanov Room
description: Cavernous gloomy ground-floor dining hall in the Dominik Mansion. Damask-curtained windows, scent of wax polish. Oak table with imperial silverware bearing the double-headed Romanov eagle: candlesticks, goblets, tureens, samovars. Portraits of assassinated Romanovs. Photograph of the five Tsarevich children from 1906.

name: Upper Brook Street
description: Street in Mayfair where the exclusive club hosting Nate Paget birthday party is located. Columned entrance with lighting. The club interior: a manned space vehicle crashed into an Edwardian country house — acid green to peony pink lighting, erotic paintings, stags antlers, futuristic sculptures.

name: St Mary's Hospital
description: Hospital in Paddington, London. Gyorgiy Peskov is admitted here under police guard after being shot and beaten at the Mayfair club.

name: Marseilles
description: City in southern France where Valentin Dominik grew up poor with his single mother, in a rough quartier. In and out of hospital for haemophilia B throughout childhood.

name: Paddington
description: West London district. Location of St Marys Hospital where Valentin is in intensive care.

name: Hertfordshire
description: County north of London. Location of the Bayford detention centre where Peskov was to be transferred.

name: Bayford detention centre
description: Detention centre in Hertfordshire.

name: Regents Canal
description: London canal visible from the ICU window at St Marys Hospital where Valentin is being treated.

name: Kensington Gardens
description: Royal park in London adjacent to Hyde Park. Eve walks here for fresh air and has her epiphany about Villanelle's message while watching crows at a cafe.

name: The Ritz Hotel
description: Luxury hotel in London. Valentin was supposed to meet Golovkin for breakfast here but did not show up.

name: The Swallow
description: Spit-and-sawdust village pub in Barnham smelling of wet dogs and beer. Eve, Villanelle, and Valentin meet here after reuniting at the shepherd's huts.

name: Regent's Park
description: Royal park in central London, near the sports complex and tennis courts.

name: Camden Town
description: District in north London. Villanelle hails a taxi here after killing a predator in Regent's Park.

name: Marylebone
description: District in central London. Villanelle claims her Russian book group met at Sussex Place here.

name: V and Eve's Mayfair Apartment
description: Top floor of a Georgian mansion on South Audley Street, Mayfair. Luxuriously comfortable but soulless. Shared by Villanelle and Eve while working for the Twelve in London. Contains weapons: Eve's Glock 9mm and Oxana's Sig Sauer P365 in the fridge, plus a ceramic stabbing knife.

name: South Audley Street
description: Street in Mayfair lined with boutiques and art galleries. Location of Villanelle and Eve's Georgian mansion apartment.

name: Bond Street
description: Luxury shopping street in central London, near Villanelle and Eve's Mayfair apartment.

name: Burlington Arcade
description: Covered shopping arcade in Mayfair. Near Villanelle and Eve's apartment on South Audley Street.

name: Hampstead Heath
description: Large ancient park in north London. Villanelle and Eve go for long walks here.

name: Parliament Hill
description: Panoramic hilltop on Hampstead Heath with a view across London. Villanelle and Eve walk here in the rain.

name: St James's Street
description: Historic street in the West End, between Piccadilly and Pall Mall. Location of the Indian Military and Sporting Club.

name: Piccadilly
description: Major thoroughfare in London's West End. St James's Street branches off Piccadilly.

name: New Bond Street
description: Luxury shopping street in Mayfair. Villanelle and Eve stop at the Ferragamo window on their walk home from the club.

name: Grafton Street
description: Street in Mayfair. An ice-blue Bentley disappears here as V and Eve walk home — someone is watching them.

name: Grafton Street Hotel
description: Hotel on Grafton Street, Mayfair. The Ismailov family stays here.

name: Berkeley Square
description: Historic square in Mayfair. Location of Caspian's casino.

name: Caspian's
description: Private members casino and club in Berkeley Square. Formerly The Trousered Ape, a fashionable 1960s nightspot. Co-owned by Nate Paget and Crispin Vale. Discreet and hedonistic atmosphere.

name: Balice and Bili's Flat
description: Two-bedroom flat on the second floor of a Victorian mansion block near Westminster Cathedral. Decorated by Bili — sporty chaos over Balice's refined taste. Home to three pet rats: Alice, Edith, and Virginia.

name: St James's Park
description: Royal park in central London. Johnny, Eve and V meet on a bench overlooking the lake to discuss the Twelve separation.

name: Naziristan
description: Fictional smallest Central Asian republic, an enclave between Tajikistan and Uzbekistan. Mostly hills and grassland. Geopolitically important for rare metals and a gas field. A good friend to Britain. Effectively controlled by Majid Ismailov.

name: Pakhtun
description: Capital of Naziristan. Location of the Ismailov family residence where Gulnara preferred to live.

name: Cambridgeshire
description: County in eastern England. Location of Newmarket and the Ismailov estate at Great Ditton.

name: Newmarket
description: Town in Cambridgeshire, headquarters of British horse racing. Majid Ismailov keeps over twenty racehorses here.

name: Longchamp
description: Paris racecourse where the Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe is held, the most prestigious flat race in the world.

name: Great Ditton
description: Majid Ismailov's estate near Newmarket. V and Eve stay here undercover as friends of Shirin to investigate the kidnapping of Budoor.

name: Sverdlovsk Oblast
description: Federal subject in the Ural Mountains region of Russia. Capital is Ekaterinburg.

name: M11 Motorway
description: Major motorway running north from London towards Cambridge and Newmarket. Eve and Villanelle drive along it to reach Great Ditton.

name: Hanover Square
description: Elegant square in Mayfair, London. Farad and Mimi have lunch at a restaurant overlooking the square.

name: Winterhall gallops
description: Training tracks (gallops) near Newmarket. Around fifty miles of grass courses designated for racehorse training, with two to three thousand horses exercising each morning. Site of Budoor's theft.

name: Riordan Racing Yard
description: Josh Riordans racing yard on the edge of Newmarket Heath. Nineteenth-century pink-brick farmhouse with three stable blocks. Around sixty horses in training, a third owned by the Ismailovs. Twenty-four-hour security with CCTV, enhanced after Budoors theft.

name: Suffolk
description: County in eastern England.

name: Bury St Edmunds
description: Town in Suffolk, England. Near the training yard inherited by Oliver Reynolds from his father Rory.

name: Norfolk
description: County in eastern England, bordering Suffolk to the south. The south-western area (Breckland) is rural and wooded.

name: Thetford
description: Town in Norfolk on the Suffolk border. Northern reference point for the ransom delivery on the A134.

name: Mundford
description: Small village in Norfolk on the A134, about six miles north of Thetford. Reference point for the ransom delivery location.

name: Breckland Forest
description: Extensive woodland in Norfolk/Suffolk between Mundford and Thetford. The kidnapper selected a remote point on the A134 here for the ransom delivery.

name: Santon Stream bridge
description: Bridge on the A134 where the Santon Stream passes beneath the road, between Mundford and Thetford. Designated ransom delivery point with a lay-by fifty yards south.

name: M11 Service Station Motel
description: Sterile, low-budget, self-service motel at an M11 service station. No reception area or signs of occupancy. Villanelle is taken here by fake police officers.

name: Brandon Road
description: Road in Breckland Forest roughly a mile west of the Santon Stream bridge. Where the kidnapper parked a Ford Focus to flee with the gold. Site of the capture.

name: Cafe Bulbul
description: Shisha lounge in Pakhtun, Naziristan. Managed by Viktor Batyrshin (his uncles business). Where Rustam Dzhuraev recruited Viktor for the Budoor kidnapping operation.

name: Pakhtun Country Club
description: Country club in Pakhtun, Naziristan. Where Farad Ismailov and Rustam Dzhuraev were childhood friends, photographed together on the tennis court as teenagers.

name: Cambridge
description: Historic university city in Cambridgeshire, eastern England.

name: Reynolds Racing Yard
description: Oliver Reynolds training yard on the Bury Road. Where Budoor was hidden under the name Frisky Lou.

name: Knightsbridge
description: Upscale district in central London, known for luxury retail and residential properties.

name: Togul
description: Mountainous area in Rundan Province, Naziristan. Site where seven environmental activists protesting against Buxor mining operations were killed.

name: Rundan Province
description: Province in Naziristan where Buxor mining company operates. Mountainous terrain with villages and tea stalls.

name: Clinique Brinvilliers
description: Private clinic in Paris. Room with a view of the Parisian skyline.

name: London Judo Association
description: Headquarters near King Cross station. Brightly lit hall with about a hundred spectators on competition nights. Bili Ilieva fights here.

name: Highgate
description: Area in north London near Hampstead Heath. V and Eve take the bus through Kentish Town up Highgate Hill to reach their new apartment.

name: V and Eve Hampstead Apartment
description: White Art Deco mansion block on the top floor near Hampstead Heath. Oak parquet floors, casement windows with endless views over the Heath. Empty when V and Eve first visit. Purchased with Twelve money (five to six million pounds). Eve calls it our home in the sky.
